steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Recall the radian-to-degree conversion factor
steps.content: To convert an angle from radians to degrees, multiply by the conversion factor below. Since $\pi$ radians equals $180^{\circ}$, we divide $180^{\circ}$ by $\pi$ to get our conversion factor.

steps.title: Apply the conversion to the given angle
steps.content:
The angle is $-\frac{5}{12}\pi$ radians. Multiply by the conversion factor to convert to degrees: $$-\frac{5}{12}\pi \cdot \frac{180^{\circ}}{\pi}$$
That confirms C is correct.
